_provenance: Pooled hazard ratios for hsCRP and CHD/stroke/vascular mortality, quoted verbatim from Kaptoge 2010 Lancet (ERFC IPD meta-analysis, PMC3162187). Per-cohort effect estimates were not extractable from PMC text (only the headline IV-pooled HRs across 54 cohorts are reported). This fixture is intentionally a 4-row single-MA fixture (4 outcomes × 1 pooled estimate each) — it exercises the engine's mixed-outcome handling and serves as a k=1 fallback test per outcome. To pool across outcomes within this fixture is scientifically wrong; use it for engine-correctness, not as a multi-cohort prognostic-factor MA.

source_meta_analysis.citation: Kaptoge S, Di Angelantonio E, Lowe G, et al. for the Emerging Risk Factors Collaboration. C-reactive protein concentration and risk of coronary heart disease, stroke, and mortality: an individual participant meta-analysis. Lancet 2010;375(9709):132-140.

outcome: CHD / stroke / vascular mortality / non-vascular mortality (4 endpoints separately)
effect_metric: HR per 1-SD higher loge(CRP) (~3-fold higher CRP on mg/L scale) after full conventional-risk-factor adjustment
design: Individual-participant data meta-analysis; 160,309 participants without prior vascular disease across 54 long-term prospective studies; 1.31M person-years; 27,769 events
finerenone_relevance: Residual inflammatory risk (hsCRP-driven MACE) is a major CV risk pathway in T2D/CKD patients. ERFC establishes the magnitude of this effect across populations and outcomes — relevant context for any cardio-renal trial including FIDELIO-DKD/FIGARO-DKD.

trials.covariates: Adjusted for age, sex, smoking status, systolic BP, history of diabetes, TC, HDL-C, alcohol use, BMI (full conventional-risk-factor adjustment per Kaptoge 2010 Methods)
Same conventional-risk-factor adjustment as CHD model

_known_data_quality_notes: These four rows are different OUTCOMES, not different studies. Pooling them across outcomes is scientifically inappropriate (they are not exchangeable). This fixture is for engine smoke-test only, demonstrating the universal-panel pipeline on real published values.
Per-cohort log-HRs across the 54 ERFC studies require the ERFC IPD or supplementary appendix (paywalled / not in PMC).
